summary: Add retention-policy.md, a human-readable companion to policy.json explaining transient/persistent durability classes, transition rules, promotion path, and agent obligations.
rationale: policy.json defines durability_classes in machine-readable form (governed by prop-20260420-001) but has no human-readable companion explaining the rules, rationale, and agent obligations in plain language. Sessions and agents must currently parse JSON to understand when to classify a capture as transient vs persistent, what the no-reclassification rule means, and how the promotion path works. retention-policy.md fills this gap without duplicating policy.json as the authoritative source — it references policy.json and explains the policy in prose. A secondary change updates memory-policy/README.md, which still describes the surface as a stub despite policy.json having been active since prop-20260420-001 — a documentation mismatch that should be corrected.
proposed_diff_summary: Add users/cici/governed-state/memory-policy/retention-policy.md with sections: Purpose, Durability Classes (transient + persistent with summary tables), Transition Rules, Promotion Path, Archived State, Agent Obligations, and Related Files. Update users/cici/governed-state/memory-policy/README.md to replace stub-era language with active-surface description listing both canonical files.
decision_notes: Created users/cici/governed-state/memory-policy/retention-policy.md as human-readable companion to policy.json. DRAFT banner removed; status set to active. Updated surface-map.json to add retention-policy.md to canonical_files for memory-policy surface. README.md already corrected in prior commit.
